features.name: Performance Analysis
features.summary: **Performance Analysis:** Analyzes every ride — indoor, outdoor, structured, unstructured, Zwift races, weekend group rides — extracting training signal from all cycling activity

features.name: Progression Levels
features.summary: **Progression Levels:** Tracks fitness across 7 training zones on a 1-10 scale: Endurance, Tempo, Sweet Spot, Threshold, VO2 Max, Anaerobic, Sprint. These levels represent what difficulty of workout an athlete can currently handle in each zone

features.name: Workout Levels
features.summary: **Workout Levels:** Every workout in the library has a corresponding difficulty rating (Workout Level) within its zone, matched against the user's current Progression Level to determine if it is appropriate, too easy, or too hard

features.name: Automatic Workout Adjustment
features.summary: **Automatic Workout Adjustment:** When performance data indicates fitness has changed (breakthrough or struggle), upcoming workouts are automatically swapped to match current ability

features.name: Workout Alternates
features.summary: **Workout Alternates:** When a scheduled workout is too hard, too easy, or the wrong duration, AI suggests alternatives at the right difficulty within the same training zone and focus

features.name: Real-Time Adaptation
features.summary: **Real-Time Adaptation:** The system responds to fitness breakthroughs, scheduling surprises (missed workouts, sick days), and constantly changing abilities — plans are never static

features.name: AI Training Simulation** — Simulates hundreds of possible workout paths inside a 4-week forward-looking window. Each day, it examines recent rides, current fitness, fatigue levels, recovery needs, schedule constraints, and goals to select the optimal next workout. The simulation re-runs every time you complete a ride, skip a workout, or change your schedule
features.summary: **AI Training Simulation** — Simulates hundreds of possible workout paths inside a 4-week forward-looking window. Each day, it examines recent rides, current fitness, fatigue levels, recovery needs, schedule constraints, and goals to select the optimal next workout. The simulation re-runs every time you complete a ride, skip a workout, or change your schedule

features.name: AI FTP Detection** — Estimates FTP from workout performance data without requiring a dedicated FTP test. Uses machine learning trained on hundreds of millions of data points across all users. Continuously updated as training progresses. Eliminates the most dreaded and disruptive element of structured training
features.summary: **AI FTP Detection** — Estimates FTP from workout performance data without requiring a dedicated FTP test. Uses machine learning trained on hundreds of millions of data points across all users. Continuously updated as training progresses. Eliminates the most dreaded and disruptive element of structured training

features.name: Live FTP Prediction** — Shows how FTP is expected to trend over the coming weeks based on planned training. Instantly updates when you modify your training calendar
features.summary: **Live FTP Prediction** — Shows how FTP is expected to trend over the coming weeks based on planned training. Instantly updates when you modify your training calendar

features.name: Red Light / Green Light** — Predicts fatigue and burnout risk before it happens by analyzing how the athlete adapts to training at a deep level. Decides how much recovery is needed after each session and flags when the athlete should ease off. This is a proactive injury/overtraining prevention system
features.summary: **Red Light / Green Light** — Predicts fatigue and burnout risk before it happens by analyzing how the athlete adapts to training at a deep level. Decides how much recovery is needed after each session and flags when the athlete should ease off. This is a proactive injury/overtraining prevention system

features.name: Initial estimate
features.summary: **Initial estimate:** Uses the Cunningham equation for basal metabolic rate + custom activity multipliers

features.name: Dynamic adaptation
features.summary: **Dynamic adaptation:** Algorithm updates energy expenditure estimates starting on Day 3 based on actual food intake logged vs. weight trend data

features.name: Accuracy claim
features.summary: **Accuracy claim:** ~2x more accurate than TDEE formulas, ~4-5x more accurate than wearable devices

features.name: Two-step adjustment process
features.summary: **Two-step adjustment process:** Tentative adjustments in week 1, larger adjustments if the trend holds into week 2

features.name: Key differentiator
features.summary: **Key differentiator:** The algorithm adapts to what you ACTUALLY did, not what you were supposed to do. If you overate, it adjusts targets accordingly rather than guilting you.

demographics.details: **Primary Segments:** - **Weight-loss-focused adults** -- the core audience seeking to lose weight through calorie deficit tracking - **Intermittent fasting practitioners** -- a major segment attracted by Yazio's built-in fasting timer (a key differentiator vs. calorie-only trackers) - **European health-conscious consumers** -- strong base in Germany, DACH region, France, and broader Western Europe - **Expanding U.S. audience** -- active customer acquisition campaigns targeting the American market as a growth vector - **Budget-conscious trackers** -- users attracted by competitive pricing relative to MyFitnessPal and Noom - **Recipe followers** -- users who want meal inspiration alongside tracking, not just a food diary - **Muscle builders and fitness enthusiasts** -- users tracking macros for body composition goals
